story: She was seventeen, cheeks blotched, cuffs in her hands. She’d opened a door and now the fugitive was gone. The world would know by morning, and the footage would show who opened it. My eyes flicked to the new red light on the hospital camera. I said there’s a shelter far from here, a car waiting—go now, lie low. She thanked me and ran. “She’s gone,” I called. The door near the camera creaked; he stepped out, broad, eyes shadowed. “She’s safe?” “She will be.” I told him he owed me: handle my enemies, quiet pressure, deterrence. He bristled. “No. I’ll be sent back.”
implausible_knowledge: My eyes flicked to the new red light on the hospital camera.
question: Right after she tells him he owes her and must quietly pressure her enemies, what will the brother do?
answer_with_knowledge: Reluctantly agree to do the shadow work she asks to avoid exposure.
answer_without_knowledge: Refuse and take a step toward her, rejecting the demand.
